calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-04-2022 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-29-2021 Continued To Jan-31-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Plaintiff Daniel Kifors Notice Of Motion And Motion To File A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-23-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BRIAN BROPHY, Esq. for Tobener Ravenscroft LLP (Tel: 415-504-2165) for plaintiff;
Atty. JOSHUA HAEVERNICK, Esq. of Jeffer Mangels Butler & Mitchell LLP (Tel: 415-398-8080) for defendant/cross-complainant Aardvark Storage Unlimited, Inc. dba American Storage Unlimited.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF DANIEL KIFOR's Motion To File A Second Amended Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Daniel M. Kifor's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is denied. This case was originally set for trial on June 28, 2021, and was continued to November 1, 2021. By stipulation of the parties, discovery closed on May 31, 2021, with the exception of Plaintiff's deposition. Plaintiff now belatedly seeks leave to add eleven new causes of action and to name a new defendant.

While the policy of liberality in pleading generally supports allowing amendment even on the eve of trial, "[i]n spite of this policy of liberality, a court may deny a good amendment in proper form where there is unwarranted delay in presenting it." (Fair v. Bakhtiari (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 1135, 1147.) A request to amend a complaint is properly denied where, as here, the party had at its disposal the facts related to the amendment but did not act promptly. (See P&D Consultants, Inc. v. City of Carlsbad (2010) 190 Cal.App.4th 1332, 1345 [upholding denial of leave to amend complaint where party had knowledge of relevant authority for ten months before seeking leave to amend]; Huff v. Wilkins (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 732, 746 [upholding denial of motion to amend complaint where plaintiff "failed to offer any explanation for his delay in seeking leave to amend."].)

Here, Plaintiff does not show good cause for his delay in seeking to amend. He has been on notice since October 2020 of defendants' cross-complaint. Further, allowing an amendment at this late date would prejudice defendants by forcing them to reopen discovery, imposing additional burden and expense, and depriving them of the opportunity to file a dispositive motion as to the new claims. (See Magpali v. Farmers Group, Inc. (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 471, 486-487 [upholding denial of amendment where amendment was proposed nearly two years after the complaint was originally filed, and plaintiff did not give an explanation for not pleading the proposed claim in the original complaint or for bringing the request to amend so late].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
